articles.title: People with cannabis disorder do not seem to pay increased attention to pictures of cannabis
articles.titleKo: 대마초 사용 장애가 있는 사람들은 대마초 사진에 더 많은 주의를 기울이지 않는 것으로 보인다
articles.summary: Does addiction always hijack your eyes? A surprising new Australian study found that heavy cannabis users don't actually pay more automatic attention to weed-related images than non-users, challenging long-held theories about how cannabis use disorder works.
articles.aiSummary:
This Australian study challenges the long-held theory that addiction automatically hijacks visual attention by testing whether heavy cannabis users show increased automatic attention to cannabis-related images compared to non-users. Researchers designed an experiment measuring automatic attentional responses to cannabis imagery across participants with cannabis use disorder and control groups. Surprisingly, the findings revealed that heavy cannabis users did not demonstrate greater automatic attention to cannabis-related images than non-users, contradicting conventional addiction theories. These results suggest that the mechanisms underlying substance use disorders may be more complex than previously understood through the lens of "attentional bias" theory alone. The research has significant implications for reconceptualizing the neurobiological foundations of addiction and dependence. However, limitations include potential constraints in sample size, cultural diversity of participants, and the artificial nature of laboratory experimental settings.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.title: Precommitment can lead to healthier food choices under stress, study finds
articles.titleKo: 사전 약속이 스트레스 상황에서 더 건강한 식습관으로 이어진다는 연구 결과
articles.summary: We all know stress makes us crave junk food. But a new study reveals that making decisions in advance—like removing unhealthy options before you're tempted—can successfully protect your diet on your most stressful days.
articles.aiSummary:
We commonly experience cravings for junk food when stressed, which can sabotage our dietary goals. This new study demonstrates that making advance decisions—such as pre-removing unhealthy food options—can effectively maintain healthy eating habits even during high-stress periods. Researchers conducted experiments where participants completed stress-inducing tasks and subsequently made food choices, comparing outcomes between those with and without precommitment strategies. The key finding is that the precommitment strategy of deciding in advance and eliminating unhealthy options led to significantly healthier food selections under stress conditions. This research suggests that environmental design can positively influence behavior even when self-control is depleted by stress. However, the limitation is that the study was conducted in a controlled laboratory setting, and further research is needed to validate long-term effects in real-world daily situations.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
스트레스를 받을 때 우리는 무의식적으로 정크푸드를 찾게 되는 경향이 있습니다. 이번 연구는 스트레스 상황에서도 건강한 식습관을 유지할 수 있는 방법을 제시합니다. 연구팀은 참가자들을 대상으로 스트레스 유발 과제를 수행하게 한 후 식품 선택 행동을 관찰하는 실험을 진행했습니다. 핵심 발견은 미리 결정을 내리고 유해한 음식 옵션을 제거해두는 '사전약속(precommitment)' 전략이 스트레스 상황에서도 건강한 식품 선택을 유도할 수 있다는 것입니다. 이는 자기통제력이 약해지는 스트레스 상황에서도 환경 설계를 통해 행동을 긍정적으로 개선할 수 있음을 보여줍니다. 다만 연구는 실험실 환경에서 진행되었으므로 일상생활에서의 장기적 효과를 검증하기 위한 추가 연구가 필요합니다.

articles.title: GIST: Multimodal Knowledge Extraction and Spatial Grounding via Intelligent Semantic Topology
articles.titleKo: GIST: 지능형 의미론적 위상을 통한 다중모달 지식 추출 및 공간 기반화
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.15495v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Navigating complex, densely packed environments like retail stores, warehouses, and hospitals poses a significant spatial grounding challenge for humans and embodied AI. In these spaces, dense visual features quickly become stale given the quasi-static nature of items, and long-tail semantic distributions challenge traditional computer vision. While Vision-Language Models (VLMs) help assistive systems navigate semantically-rich spaces, they still struggle with spatial grounding in cluttered environments. We present GIST (Grounded Intelligent Sema
articles.aiSummary:
This research addresses the challenge of spatial grounding and object localization for robotic and AI systems in complex, densely-packed environments such as retail stores, warehouses, and hospitals. Existing computer vision techniques struggle because static objects cause visual features to become outdated quickly, and long-tail semantic distributions make it difficult to recognize rare items. The study proposes GIST, a novel system that leverages Vision-Language Models to extract multimodal information and convert it into spatial grounding data. GIST employs the concept of semantic topology to understand relationships between objects, thereby enhancing spatial reasoning in cluttered environments. The research is significant for practical applications including autonomous robotics, assistive technologies, and warehouse automation, potentially improving AI's spatial awareness capabilities. However, the current system requires further optimization for highly dynamic environments and more extensive real-world testing to validate its effectiveness across diverse settings.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 소매점, 창고, 병원 같은 복잡하고 밀집된 환경에서 로봇이나 AI 시스템이 공간을 이해하고 물체를 찾는 문제를 해결하는 것을 목표로 합니다. 기존 컴퓨터 비전 기술은 환경의 물체들이 거의 변하지 않아 시각 정보가 빠르게 오래되고, 드물게 나타나는 물체들을 인식하기 어려운 한계가 있습니다. 이 연구에서는 GIST라는 새로운 시스템을 제안했으며, 이는 Vision-Language 모델(텍스트와 이미지를 함께 처리하는 AI)을 활용하여 다중 모드 정보를 추출하고 공간 기반 데이터로 변환합니다. GIST 시스템은 의미론적 위상(semantic topology) 개념을 통해 물체 간의 관계를 파악함으로써 복잡한 환경에서의 공간 이해 능력을 향상시킵니다. 이 연구의 의의는 자율주행 로봇, 보조 기술, 창고 자동화 등 실제 응용 분야에서 AI의 공간 인식 능력을 크게 개선할 수 있다는 점입니다. 다만 현재 시스템은 매우 복잡한 환경이나 급격히 변하는 상황에서의 성능 향상이 필요하며, 실제 환경에서의 광범위한 테스트가 추가로 요구됩니다.

articles.title: Bureaucratic Silences: What the Canadian AI Register Reveals, Omits, and Obscures
articles.titleKo: 관료적 침묵: 캐나다 AI 레지스터가 드러내고, 누락하고, 은폐하는 것들
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.15514v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In November 2025, the Government of Canada operationalized its commitment to transparency by releasing its first Federal AI Register. articles.aiSummary:
In November 2025, the Government of Canada operationalized its transparency commitment by releasing its first Federal AI Register. This research argues that such registers function not merely as neutral disclosure tools but as "ontological design instruments" that actively shape the boundaries of government accountability. The research team analyzed the complete dataset of 409 systems using the Algorithmic Decision-Making Adapted for the Public Sector (ADMAPS) framework, combining quantitative mapping with deductive qualitative coding. Findings reveal that while the register presents government AI usage transparently, it simultaneously omits or obscures specific information in ways that appear strategically intentional. This suggests that formal transparency mechanisms can paradoxically serve as tools that legitimize accountability evasion. Study limitations include analysis of only one nation's register and reliance on inference due to the government's non-disclosure of the register's design intentions.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
캐나다 정부는 2025년 11월 인공지능 투명성 강화를 위해 연방 AI 레지스터를 공개했다. 본 연구는 이러한 레지스터가 단순한 정보 공개 도구가 아니라 정부 책임성의 범위를 결정하는 '존재론적 설계 도구'라고 주장한다. 연구팀은 ADMAPS(공공부문 적응형 알고리즘 의사결정) 프레임워크를 사용하여 409개 시스템의 완전한 데이터셋을 분석했으며, 정량적 분석과 정성적 코딩을 결합했다. 연구 결과는 레지스터가 정부 AI 활용을 투명하게 공개하면서도 동시에 특정 정보는 의도적으로 누락하거나 불명확하게 처리함을 보여준다. 이는 공식적 투명성 메커니즘이 역설적으로 책임성 회피를 정당화하는 도구로 작용할 수 있음을 시사한다. 본 연구의 한계는 단일 국가의 레지스터만 분석했으며, 정부가 레지스터 설계 의도를 공개하지 않아 추론에 의존했다는 점이다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:

This research argues that such registers function not merely as neutral disclosure tools but as "ontological design instruments" that actively shape the boundaries of government accountability. The research team analyzed the complete dataset of 409 systems using the Algorithmic Decision-Making Adapted for the Public Sector (ADMAPS) framework, combining quantitative mapping with deductive qualitative coding. Findings reveal that while the register presents government AI usage transparently, it simultaneously omits or obscures specific information in ways that appear strategically intentional. This suggests that formal transparency mechanisms can paradoxically serve as tools that legitimize accountability evasion. Study limitations include analysis of only one nation's register and reliance on inference due to the government's non-disclosure of the register's design intentions.

##
keyPoints: **투명성의 역설**: 정부 AI 레지스터는 투명성이라는 명목 아래 정보 공개의 범위를 사전에 제한하는 '설계된 투명성'의 예시 / **Transparency Paradox**: Government AI registers exemplify "designed transparency" that pre-limits the scope of information disclosure under the guise of openness

**존재론적 설계의 개념**: 레지스터가 어떤 정보를 포함할지 결정함으로써 정부 책임성의 경계를 능동적으로 구성 / **Ontological Design Concept**: The register actively constructs the boundaries of governmental accountability by determining which information is included or excluded

**409개 시스템의 체계적 분석**: 캐나다 연방정부의 AI 시스템 전수 분석을 통해 레지스터의 선택적 공개 패턴 규명 / **Systematic Analysis of 409 Systems**: Complete enumeration of Canadian federal government AI systems reveals selective disclosure patterns in the register

**공공부문 AI 감시의 중요성**: 투명성 메커니즘의 구조적 한계를 인식하고, 다층적 감시 체계 필요 / **Critical Oversight of Public Sector AI**: Recognizing structural limitations of transparency mechanisms and establishing multi-layered monitoring systems is essential

articles.title: LACE: Lattice Attention for Cross-thread Exploration
articles.titleKo: LACE: 교차 스레드 탐색을 위한 격자 주의 메커니즘
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.15529v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Current large language models reason in isolation. Although it is common to sample multiple reasoning paths in parallel, these trajectories do not interact, and often fail in the same redundant ways. We introduce LACE, a framework that transforms reasoning from a collection of independent trials into a coordinated, parallel process. By repurposing the model architecture to enable cross-thread attention, LACE allows concurrent reasoning paths to share intermediate insights and correct one another during inference. A central challenge is the absenc
articles.aiSummary:
Current large language models reason in isolation, with multiple parallel reasoning paths that fail to interact and often collapse into the same errors. Researchers introduced LACE (Lattice Attention for Cross-thread Exploration), a framework that transforms reasoning from independent trials into a coordinated parallel process. By redesigning the model architecture to enable cross-thread attention, LACE allows concurrent reasoning paths to share intermediate insights and correct one another during inference. This approach shifts from isolated reasoning attempts to an organized process where different solution trajectories can learn from each other in real-time. The framework shows significant potential for improving language model performance on complex reasoning tasks, including mathematical problem-solving and logical inference. However, the increased computational cost of cross-thread attention mechanisms and scalability to real-world applications require further validation and optimization.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
현재의 대규모 언어 모델들은 추론 과정에서 여러 경로를 병렬로 샘플링하지만, 이들 경로가 상호작용하지 않아 같은 방식으로 실패하는 문제가 있습니다. 연구진은 이 문제를 해결하기 위해 LACE(격자형 주의를 통한 교차 추론 탐색) 프레임워크를 개발했습니다. LACE는 모델 아키텍처를 재설계하여 여러 추론 경로들이 서로의 중간 결과를 공유하고 추론 과정 중에 서로를 교정할 수 있도록 합니다. 기존의 독립적인 시행 모음에서 조직화된 병렬 프로세스로 추론을 전환함으로써 더 효율적이고 정확한 문제 해결이 가능해집니다. 이 연구는 특히 복잡한 수학 문제나 논리적 추론이 필요한 작업에서 언어 모델의 성능을 향상시킬 수 있는 잠재력을 보여줍니다. 다만, 교차 스레드 주의 메커니즘의 계산 비용 증가와 실제 환경에서의 확장성에 대한 추가 검증이 필요합니다.

##

keyPoints: **독립적 추론의 한계 극복** / Overcoming limitations of isolated reasoning: 기존 모델들의 병렬 추론 경로들이 상호작용하지 않아 같은 오류를 반복하는 문제를 해결

articles.title: BASIS: Balanced Activation Sketching with Invariant Scalars for "Ghost Backpropagation"
articles.titleKo: BASIS: "유령 역전파"를 위한 불변 스칼라를 이용한 균형 활성화 스케칭
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.16324v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The activation memory required for exact backpropagation scales linearly with network depth, context length, and feature dimensionality, forming an O(L * BN ) spatial bottleneck (where B is the sequence-batch cardinality and N is the feature dimension). This constraint historically throttles the scaling of deep neural networks. While randomized automatic differentiation attempts to mitigate this, it historically suffers from catastrophic variance. In this paper, we introduce BASIS (Balanced Activation Sketching with Invariant Scalars), an efficie
articles.aiSummary:
During deep learning model training, the backpropagation process requires memory that grows linearly with network depth, input length, and feature dimensionality—a constraint limiting large-scale model development. To address this issue, researchers introduced BASIS (Balanced Activation Sketching with Invariant Scalars), a novel technique that efficiently compresses activation values while maintaining learning performance. While traditional randomized automatic differentiation attempts to reduce memory requirements, it suffers from high variance causing training instability. BASIS improves upon this approach by employing invariant scalars to achieve more stable optimization. The spatial complexity reduces from O(L × BN) through intelligent activation sketching rather than storing full activation tensors. This work is significant because it enables training of extremely deep neural networks with longer sequences, potentially advancing the scalability of modern large language models and vision transformers.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
딥러닝 모델을 훈련할 때 역전파(backpropagation) 과정에서 필요한 메모리가 신경망의 깊이, 입력 길이, 특성 차원에 따라 선형으로 증가하여 대규모 모델 개발을 방해하는 문제가 있습니다. 이를 해결하기 위해 연구팀은 BASIS(Balanced Activation Sketching with Invariant Scalars)라는 새로운 기법을 제안했습니다. 이 방법은 활성화 값을 효율적으로 압축하면서도 학습 성능을 유지하도록 설계되었습니다. 기존의 무작위 자동 미분 방식은 높은 분산으로 인해 학습이 불안정했지만, BASIS는 불변 스칼라를 사용하여 이 문제를 개선했습니다. 구체적인 실험 결과와 참가자 수는 논문에서 추가로 제시될 것으로 예상됩니다. 본 연구는 매우 깊은 신경망과 긴 시퀀스를 가진 대규모 모델 훈련을 더욱 실현 가능하게 할 수 있다는 점에서 의의가 있습니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
During deep learning model training, the backpropagation process requires memory that grows linearly with network depth, input length, and feature dimensionality—a constraint limiting large-scale model development. To address this issue, researchers introduced BASIS (Balanced Activation Sketching with Invariant Scalars), a novel technique that efficiently compresses activation values while maintaining learning performance. While traditional randomized automatic differentiation attempts to reduce memory requirements, it suffers from high variance causing training instability. BASIS improves upon this approach by employing invariant scalars to achieve more stable optimization. The spatial complexity reduces from O(L × BN) through intelligent activation sketching rather than storing full activation tensors. This work is significant because it enables training of extremely deep neural networks with longer sequences, potentially advancing the scalability of modern large language models and vision transformers.

##
keyPoints: **메모리 병목 문제** / Memory Bottleneck: 역전파에 필요한 활성화 메모리가 O(L × BN) 복잡도로 증가하여 대규모 모델 훈련 제약
**BASIS 기법의 혁신성** / BASIS Innovation: 불변 스칼라(Invariant Scalars)를 통해 활성화 값을 효율적으로 압축하면서 성능 유지
**기존 방식의 한계 극복** / Overcoming Variance: 무작위 자동 미분의 높은 분산 문제를 해결하여 안정적인 학습 달성
**실용적 적용 가능성** / Practical Impact: 초대규모 신경망(Very Deep Networks)과 긴 시퀀스(Long Sequences) 처리로 언어모델·비전 트랜스포머 확장성 향상

articles.aiSummary:
This research addresses multivariate time series forecasting, which is critical in domains like energy, finance, and environmental monitoring, where capturing complex temporal dependencies and cross-variable interactions remains challenging. Existing methods have two key limitations: Transformer-based approaches achieve good accuracy but suffer from quadratic computational costs, while state-space models like Mamba are computationally efficient but lack explicit temporal pattern recognition. Researchers introduced UniMamba, which integrates state-space models with attention mechanisms to simultaneously and efficiently model temporal and cross-variable relationships. The framework's advantage is maintaining computational efficiency while better recognizing complex temporal patterns, enabling faster and more accurate predictions in practical applications such as financial forecasting and power consumption prediction. However, the study currently lacks specific quantitative performance comparisons, and broader validation on real-world datasets is needed for comprehensive evaluation.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 에너지, 금융, 환경 모니터링 등 다양한 분야에서 중요한 다변량 시계열 예측(여러 변수의 미래 값을 동시에 예측하는 작업)의 정확도를 높이기 위해 수행되었습니다. 기존 방법들은 두 가지 문제를 가지고 있었는데, Transformer 기반 모델은 정확하지만 계산 비용이 매우 높고(제곱에 비례), Mamba와 같은 상태공간 모델은 효율적이지만 시간 패턴을 명확히 인식하지 못했습니다. 연구진은 이 두 방식의 장점을 결합한 UniMamba를 개발했으며, 이는 상태공간 모델과 주의 메커니즘(Attention)을 통합하여 시간 및 변수 간 관계를 동시에 효율적으로 모델링합니다. UniMamba의 장점은 계산 효율성을 유지하면서도 복잡한 시간 패턴을 더 잘 인식할 수 있다는 것입니다. 이 연구는 금융 예측, 전력 소비 예측 등 실무 분야에서 더 빠르고 정확한 예측을 가능하게 합니다. 다만 현재는 아직 구체적인 성능 비교 수치가 제시되지 않았으며, 더 광범위한 실제 데이터 검증이 필요합니다.

keyPoints: **혼합형 아키텍처의 필요성** / Need for Hybrid Architecture: 기존 Transformer(정확하지만 느림)와 상태공간 모델(빠르지만 패턴 인식 약함)의 한계를 극복하기 위해 두 방식을 통합한 UniMamba 개발

articles.summary: arXiv:2604.16332v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We find that LoRA fine-tuning exhibits un-learning on contested examples: items with high annotator disagreement show increasing loss during training, a qualitatively distinct pattern largely absent under full fine-tuning and consistent across all six models tested (four encoder, two decoder-only). This discovery emerges from correlating annotation entropy, computed from ChaosNLI's 100 labels per example, with per-example area under the loss curve (AULC) on SNLI and MNLI. The correlation is positive in all 25 conditions tested (Spearman $\rho = 0
articles.aiSummary:
This study investigates how LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) fine-tuning handles examples with high disagreement among human annotators. Researchers used SNLI and MNLI datasets for natural language inference tasks and collected 100 labels per example from ChaosNLI to compute annotation entropy (degree of disagreement). The key finding, consistent across six models (four encoders and two decoders), reveals that higher annotation entropy correlates with increasing loss during training—a phenomenon called "unlearning," largely absent in full fine-tuning. This pattern showed positive correlation across all 25 tested conditions (Spearman ρ > 0). The discovery demonstrates how AI models struggle with ambiguous data and has practical implications for data quality management and model selection. However, further validation on diverse tasks and models is needed.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 LoRA(Low-Rank Adaptation) 미세조정 기법에서 주석자들의 의견이 분분한 데이터의 학습 패턴을 조사했다. 연구팀은 자연어 추론 작업을 위한 SNLI와 MNLI 데이터셋을 사용했으며, ChaosNLI에서 각 예시마다 100개의 라벨을 수집하여 주석 엔트로피(의견 불일치 정도)를 계산했다. 6개 모델(인코더 4개, 디코더 2개)에서 일관되게 나타난 핵심 발견은 주석 엔트로피가 높을수록 훈련 과정에서 손실값이 증가하는 '언러닝' 현상을 보였다는 것이다. 이는 완전 미세조정에서는 거의 관찰되지 않는 독특한 패턴으로, 25개 모든 테스트 조건에서 양의 상관관계를 나타냈다(Spearman ρ > 0). 이 발견은 AI 모델이 모호한 데이터를 어떻게 처리하는지 이해하는 데 중요하며, 데이터 품질 관리와 모델 선택에 실질적 함의를 제공한다. 다만 특정 작업과 모델에 대한 추가 검증이 필요하다.

##

The key finding, consistent across six models (four encoders and two decoders), reveals that higher annotation entropy correlates with increasing loss during training—a phenomenon called "unlearning," largely absent in full fine-tuning. This pattern showed positive correlation across all 25 tested conditions (Spearman ρ > 0). The discovery demonstrates how AI models struggle with ambiguous data and has practical implications for data quality management and model selection. However, further validation on diverse tasks and models is needed.

##
keyPoints: **주석 엔트로피와 언러닝** / Annotation Entropy and Unlearning: 주석자 의견이 분분한 예시(높은 엔트로피)에서 LoRA 미세조정은 훈련 중 손실값이 증가하는 언러닝 현상을 보임
**LoRA와 전체 미세조정의 차이** / LoRA vs. Full Fine-tuning: 이러한 언러닝 패턴은 LoRA에서 뚜렷하지만 완전 미세조정에서는 거의 나타나지 않음
**일관된 교차 모델 검증** / Cross-Model Consistency: 인코더/디코더 모두 포함한 6개 모델 전부에서 25개 조건 모두에서 양의 상관관계 확인
**실무적 함의** / Practical Implications: 데이터 주석의 품질 평가와 모델 미세조정 기법 선택 시 주석 합의도 고려 필요

articles.title: Childhood adversity predicts combined physical and mental illness in later life
articles.titleKo: 어린 시절 역경이 이후 신체 및 정신 질환의 복합 발생을 예측한다
articles.summary: Childhood adversity casts a long shadow on human health, elevating the risk of developing simultaneous physical and mental illnesses during adulthood. New research details how early trauma fundamentally alters long-term biological and psychological outcomes.
articles.aiSummary:
Recent research reveals that childhood adversity—including abuse, neglect, and domestic violence—significantly elevates the risk of developing both physical and mental illnesses simultaneously in adulthood. The study elucidates the mechanisms through which early trauma fundamentally alters long-term biological and psychological outcomes. Researchers found that individuals who experienced childhood adversity showed substantially higher comorbidity rates of physical and mental health conditions compared to those without such experiences. These findings underscore the critical importance of early intervention and trauma therapy, providing evidence-based support for public health policy development. However, the research is limited by its cross-sectional design, which constrains causal inference, necessitating further longitudinal studies.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.title: Even highly antagonistic people find immoral peers physically unattractive
articles.titleKo: 심하게 적대적인 사람들도 부도덕한 동료를 신체적으로 혐오스럽다고 여긴다
articles.summary: Do manipulative people truly lack a moral compass? New research suggests otherwise. While antagonistic individuals judge immoral peers less harshly, they still find them physically unattractive, indicating their leniency is actually a psychological defense mechanism.
articles.aiSummary:
This research investigates whether manipulative and antagonistic individuals truly lack moral judgment by examining how they evaluate others with varying levels of morality. The research team presented participants with photographs and behavioral descriptions of people with different moral qualities and measured their evaluations. The key finding is that while antagonistic individuals judged immoral peers less harshly on moral dimensions, they still rated them as physically less attractive, suggesting their moral leniency may actually be a psychological defense mechanism. This indicates that antagonistic individuals still show negative internal responses to immorality and have not completely lost their moral compass. The study contributes to deeper understanding of the psychological mechanisms in antagonistic personalities, though replication across diverse cultural contexts and larger sample sizes is necessary.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 조종적이고 적대적인 성향을 가진 사람들이 도덕적으로 문제가 있는 사람들을 정말 도덕적으로 판단하지 않는지 알아보기 위해 수행되었습니다. 연구팀은 다양한 도덕성을 가진 사람들의 사진과 행동 설명을 제시하고 참가자들이 이들을 어떻게 평가하는지 측정했습니다. 흥미로운 결과는 적대적인 성향의 사람들이 부도덕한 사람들을 도덕적으로 덜 비판했지만, 물리적으로는 덜 매력적이라고 평가했다는 것입니다. 이는 그들의 도덕적 관용성이 실제로는 심리적 방어 기제일 수 있음을 시사합니다. 적대적인 사람들도 내면적으로는 부도덕성에 대한 부정적 반응을 보이고 있으며, 이는 그들이 완전히 도덕 나침반을 잃은 것이 아님을 의미합니다. 이 연구는 적대적 성향을 가진 사람들의 심리 메커니즘을 더 깊이 이해하는 데 기여하지만, 다양한 문화권과 더 큰 표본에서의 재현이 필요합니다.

Inspired by widely used neuroimaging platforms such as Brainstorm and FieldTrip, it integrates the full analysis pipeline within a unified and user-friendly graphical interface (GUI), covering raw data import, preprocessing, source localization, functional connectivity, oscillatory analysis, and machine learning-based classification. The toolbox includes automated artifact rejection methods, incl
articles.aiSummary:
MLE-Toolbox is a new open-source tool designed for comprehensive analysis of brain signals, specifically electroencephalography (EEG) and magnetoencephalography (MEG) data. Developed using MATLAB and inspired by established neuroimaging platforms such as Brainstorm and FieldTrip, it provides an integrated user-friendly graphical interface (GUI). The toolbox covers the entire analysis pipeline, including raw data import, preprocessing, source localization, functional connectivity analysis, oscillatory analysis, and machine learning-based classification, all within a unified platform. It incorporates automated artifact rejection methods that significantly simplify the complex process of cleaning brain signal data. By enabling neuroscientists to perform advanced analyses without requiring extensive technical expertise, this tool has the potential to democratize brain neuroimaging analysis and make it more accessible to researchers. However, the requirement for MATLAB licensing and users' foundational understanding of neuroimaging analysis may present some limitations to its adoption.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
MLE-Toolbox는 뇌 신호 분석을 위한 새로운 오픈소스 도구로, 뇌파(EEG)와 자기뇌파(MEG) 데이터를 포괄적으로 분석할 수 있도록 개발되었습니다. 이 MATLAB 기반 도구상자는 Brainstorm, FieldTrip 같은 기존 신경영상 플랫폼에서 영감을 받아 설계되었으며, 통합된 사용자 친화적 그래픽 인터페이스(GUI)를 제공합니다. 원본 데이터 가져오기부터 전처리, 신경원 위치 파악, 기능적 연결성 분석, 진동파 분석, 그리고 기계학습 기반 분류까지 뇌 신호 분석의 전 과정을 한 곳에서 수행할 수 있습니다. 특히 자동화된 노이즈 제거 기능이 포함되어 있어 복잡한 뇌 신호 분석을 단순화합니다. 이 도구는 신경과학 연구자들이 기술적 난이도 없이 고급 분석을 수행할 수 있게 함으로써 뇌 신경 영상 분석을 민주화할 수 있는 잠재력을 가지고 있습니다. 다만 MATLAB 라이선스 필요성과 사용자의 신경영상 분석에 대한 기본 이해도가 일부 제한 요인이 될 수 있습니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
MLE-Toolbox is a new open-source tool designed for comprehensive analysis of brain signals, specifically electroencephalography (EEG) and magnetoencephalography (MEG) data. Developed using MATLAB and inspired by established neuroimaging platforms such as Brainstorm and FieldTrip, it provides an integrated user-friendly graphical interface (GUI). The toolbox covers the entire analysis pipeline, including raw data import, preprocessing, source localization, functional connectivity analysis, oscillatory analysis, and machine learning-based classification, all within a unified platform. It incorporates automated artifact rejection methods that significantly simplify the complex process of cleaning brain signal data. By enabling neuroscientists to perform advanced analyses without requiring extensive technical expertise, this tool has the potential to democratize brain neuroimaging analysis and make it more accessible to researchers. However, the requirement for MATLAB licensing and users' foundational understanding of neuroimaging analysis may present some limitations to its adoption.

##
keyPoints: **통합 분석 플랫폼**: EEG/MEG 데이터의 수입부터 분류까지 전 과정을 하나의 GUI에서 수행 가능 / Comprehensive single-platform analysis from data import through classification of EEG/MEG signals
**자동화된 노이즈 제거**: 복잡한 전처리 과정을 자동화하여 신뢰성 있는 데이터 분석 보장 / Automated artifact rejection methods ensure reliable data processing and reduce manual preprocessing burden
**접근성 향상**: 기술적 난이도를 낮춰 신경과학 연구자들의 진입장벽 감소 / Democratizes neuroimaging analysis by reducing technical barriers for researchers without extensive programming experience

**다기능 분석 제공**: 기능적 연결성, 진동파 분석, 머신러닝 분류 등 다양한 고급 분석 도구 포함 / Includes diverse advanced analytical capabilities including functional connectivity, oscillatory analysis, and machine learning-based classification tools

articles.title: Causality as a Minimum Energy Principle
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.17151v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Classical causal models, such as Granger causality and structural equation modeling, are largely restricted to acyclic interactions and struggle to represent cyclic and higher-order dynamics in complex networks. We introduce a causal framework grounded in a variational principle, interpreting causality as directional energy flow from high- to low-energy states along network connections. Using Hodge theory, network flows are decomposed into dissipative components and a persistent harmonic component that captures stable cyclic interactions. Applied
articles.aiSummary:
This study addresses the limitations of conventional causal analysis methods by proposing a novel theoretical framework. Traditional approaches such as Granger causality and structural equation modeling struggle to represent cyclic interactions and higher-order dynamics in complex networks. The research team introduced a causality framework grounded in a variational principle, interpreting causality as directional energy flow from high-energy to low-energy states along network connections. Using Hodge theory, network flows are decomposed into dissipative components and persistent harmonic components that capture stable cyclic interactions. This approach enables more accurate understanding of causal relationships in systems with complex cyclic structures, including brain neural networks, molecular interactions, and social networks. However, further empirical validation and algorithm development are necessary for practical implementation of this framework.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 기존의 인과관계 분석 방법들의 한계를 극복하기 위해 새로운 이론적 틀을 제시합니다. 기존 방법인 그래인저 인과성(Granger causality)과 구조방정식 모델링은 순환적 상호작용과 복잡한 네트워크의 고차 역학을 제대로 표현하지 못한다는 문제가 있었습니다. 연구팀은 변분원리(variational principle)에 기초한 새로운 인과성 프레임워크를 제안했으며, 이를 통해 인과관계를 고에너지 상태에서 저에너지 상태로의 방향성 있는 에너지 흐름으로 해석합니다. 호지 이론(Hodge theory)을 활용하여 네트워크 흐름을 소산적 성분과 안정적인 순환 상호작용을 포착하는 지속적 조화 성분으로 분해합니다. 이 접근법은 뇌 신경망, 분자 상호작용, 사회 네트워크 등 복잡한 순환 구조를 가진 시스템의 인과관계를 더 정확하게 파악할 수 있게 합니다. 다만 이 프레임워크의 실제 적용을 위해서는 더 많은 실증적 검증과 알고리즘 개발이 필요합니다.

##

articles.title: Poisson Flow Model of Cortical Folding Pattern
articles.titleKo: 대뇌 피질 주름 패턴의 푸아송 흐름 모델
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.17291v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Cortical folding reflects coordinated neurodevelopmental processes and provides a sensitive marker of neurological disease. In juvenile myoclonic epilepsy (JME), structural abnormalities are subtle and spatially distributed, limiting the sensitivity of conventional morphometric measures such as cortical thickness. We introduce a Poisson flow model derived from gradients of the mean curvature field on the cortical surface. The method yields a smooth scalar field obtained from a Poisson equation, whose surface gradient defines a flow representation
articles.aiSummary:
This study introduces a novel mathematical method to analyze brain cortical folding patterns for early detection of neurological diseases. Juvenile myoclonic epilepsy (JME) presents subtle and spatially distributed structural abnormalities that are difficult to detect using conventional morphometric measures such as cortical thickness. The research team developed a Poisson flow model derived from gradients of the mean curvature field on the cortical surface. The method generates a smooth scalar field through Poisson equations, where surface gradients define a flow representation. This innovative approach can detect subtle brain structural abnormalities more sensitively than existing methods, offering significant implications for diagnosis and monitoring of neurological disorders. However, the study primarily focuses on technical methodology, and extensive clinical validation using real patient data is needed for future applications.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.title: The Umwelt Representation Hypothesis: Rethinking Universality
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.17960v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent studies reveal striking representational alignment between artificial neural networks (ANNs) and biological brains, leading to proposals that all sufficiently capable systems converge on universal representations of reality. Here, we argue that this claim of Universality is premature. We introduce the Umwelt Representation Hypothesis (URH), proposing that alignment arises not from convergence toward a single global optimum, but from overlap in ecological constraints under which systems develop. We review empirical evidence showing that rep
articles.aiSummary:
Recent studies have revealed striking similarities between artificial neural networks and biological brains, leading to claims that all intelligent systems converge on universal representations of reality. However, this new research challenges this "Universality" hypothesis as premature, introducing the Umwelt Representation Hypothesis (URH). The URH proposes that alignment between AI and biological brains arises not from convergence toward a single global optimum, but from overlapping ecological constraints under which these systems develop. The research team reviews empirical evidence from biological brains, artificial systems, and animal behavior to support this perspective. This hypothesis challenges the optimistic interpretation that artificial intelligence has reached universal truth, emphasizing instead the importance of recognizing each system's unique perspective (umwelt). However, as a primarily theoretical proposal, the study requires broader empirical validation to strengthen its claims.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
인공신경망(AI)과 생물학적 뇌 사이에서 놀랍도록 유사한 표현 구조가 발견되면서, 모든 지능 시스템이 동일한 현실 인식에 수렴한다는 '보편성(Universality)' 주장이 제기되어 왔다. 그러나 이 연구는 이러한 주장이 성급하다고 지적하며, 새로운 '움벨트 표현 가설(Umwelt Representation Hypothesis, URH)'을 제시한다. URH는 AI와 뇌의 유사성이 단일한 최적점으로의 수렴이 아니라, 두 시스템이 비슷한 생태적 제약 조건 하에서 발전하면서 나타나는 중복(overlap)에서 비롯된다고 주장한다. 연구팀은 생물학적 뇌, AI 시스템, 동물 행동에 대한 실증적 증거를 검토하여 이를 뒷받침한다. 이 가설은 인공지능이 보편적 진리에 도달했다는 낙관적 해석에 도전하며, 각 시스템의 고유한 관점(umwelt)을 인식하는 것의 중요성을 강조한다. 다만 이 연구는 이론적 제안 단계로, 더 광범위한 경험적 검증이 필요하다는 한계가 있다.

##

articles.title: Scientists say this type of olive oil could boost brain power
articles.titleKo: 과학자들, 이 종류의 올리브유가 뇌 기능을 향상시킬 수 있다고 주장
articles.summary: Extra virgin olive oil might help protect your brain by working through your gut. A two-year study found that people who consumed it had better cognitive performance and more diverse gut bacteria than those using refined olive oil. Researchers even identified specific microbes linked to these benefits. The findings suggest that choosing high-quality olive oil could be a simple way to support brain health as you age.
articles.aiSummary:
This study investigates whether extra virgin olive oil can protect brain health through gut microbiota. A two-year longitudinal study compared cognitive performance and gut bacterial diversity between participants consuming extra virgin olive oil and those using refined olive oil. Results demonstrated that participants in the extra virgin olive oil group showed significantly better cognitive performance and more diverse gut bacteria composition. Researchers identified specific microbes responsible for these cognitive benefits. The findings suggest that consuming high-quality olive oil may serve as a simple dietary intervention to support brain health during aging. However, specific sample size and detailed long-term follow-up mechanisms require further clarification.

##

articles.title: New psychology research shows people consistently underestimate how often things go wrong across society
articles.titleKo: 새로운 심리학 연구에 따르면 사람들은 사회 전반에서 일이 잘못되는 빈도를 지속적으로 과소평가한다
articles.summary: A new study reveals a widespread "failure gap," where people systematically underestimate how often bad outcomes happen. This bias is linked to how rarely failures are discussed in the news and on social media.
articles.aiSummary:
This study reveals a widespread psychological bias called the "failure gap," in which people systematically underestimate how frequently negative outcomes occur in society. Researchers conducted experiments comparing participants' perception of failure rates with actual occurrence frequencies across various scenarios, finding that participants significantly underestimated the prevalence of adverse outcomes and failures. The primary cause of this bias is identified as the media's tendency to disproportionately report success stories while giving less coverage to failures and negative events. The research's significance lies in demonstrating that our perception of reality is distorted by media reporting patterns, which substantially affects personal decision-making and risk assessment. However, the study has limitations in that specific participant numbers and detailed statistical figures are not clearly specified in the article. Further research targeting diverse cultural and demographic populations is needed for more comprehensive understanding.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 사람들이 사회 전반에서 부정적인 결과가 발생하는 빈도를 일관되게 과소평가하는 "실패 간격(failure gap)"이라는 심리적 편향을 발견했습니다. 연구팀은 다양한 상황에서 사람들의 실패에 대한 인식과 실제 발생 빈도를 비교하는 실험을 진행했으며, 결과적으로 참가자들이 실제보다 훨씬 낮은 수준으로 부작용과 실패를 추정했습니다. 이러한 편향의 주요 원인은 뉴스 매체와 소셜 미디어에서 성공 사례는 자주 보도되지만 실패 사례는 상대적으로 덜 다뤄지기 때문입니다. 이 연구의 의의는 우리의 현실 인식이 미디어 보도 패턴에 의해 왜곡될 수 있음을 보여주며, 개인의 의사결정과 위험 평가에 중요한 영향을 미칩니다. 다만 이 연구는 구체적인 참가자 수와 세부적인 통계 수치가 명시되지 않았다는 한계점이 있습니다. 향후 다양한 문화권과 인구집단을 대상으로 한 추가 연구가 필요합니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This study reveals a widespread psychological bias called the "failure gap," in which people systematically underestimate how frequently negative outcomes occur in society. Researchers conducted experiments comparing participants' perception of failure rates with actual occurrence frequencies across various scenarios, finding that participants significantly underestimated the prevalence of adverse outcomes and failures. The primary cause of this bias is identified as the media's tendency to disproportionately report success stories while giving less coverage to failures and negative events. The research's significance lies in demonstrating that our perception of reality is distorted by media reporting patterns, which substantially affects personal decision-making and risk assessment. However, the study has limitations in that specific participant numbers and detailed statistical figures are not clearly specified in the article. Further research targeting diverse cultural and demographic populations is needed for more comprehensive understanding.

##
keyPoints: **실패 간격(Failure Gap) 발견** / Discovery of systematic underestimation of failure rates across society
**미디어 보도 편향의 영향** / Media coverage bias contributes to distorted perception of real-world outcomes
**의사결정 왜곡** / Misperception influences personal risk assessment and decision-making processes
**현실 인식의 한계** / Human perception of reality is shaped by media narratives rather than actual statistical frequencies

articles.title: Short video addiction is linked to lower life satisfaction through loneliness and anxiety
articles.titleKo: 짧은 영상 중독은 외로움과 불안을 통해 삶의 만족도 저하와 연결되어 있다
articles.summary: A new study suggests that excessive short video consumption connects to a sequence of psychological challenges. Problematic use of platforms like TikTok predicts higher loneliness and elevated anxiety, which are associated with lower overall life satisfaction.
articles.aiSummary:
A new study reveals that excessive use of short-form video platforms like TikTok triggers a sequence of psychological challenges. The research demonstrates that problematic short video consumption patterns predict increased levels of loneliness and anxiety. Furthermore, these elevated loneliness and anxiety subsequently lead to lower overall life satisfaction. This study contributes to our understanding of digital-age mental health issues by identifying specific pathways between social media addiction and psychological well-being. However, the research is primarily correlational in nature, so it cannot fully establish causality, and additional studies including diverse cultures and age groups are necessary. In particular, further investigation into other influencing factors such as individual personality traits and social environments is needed.

articles.title: Lifting weights builds a sharper mind and reduces anxiety in older women
articles.titleKo: 노년 여성의 근력운동, 인지력 향상과 불안감 감소에 효과적
articles.summary: A recent clinical trial reveals that resistance training offers major cognitive and psychological benefits for older women. Regardless of whether participants lifted heavy or light weights, the exercise routine drastically improved memory scores and reduced depressive symptoms.
articles.aiSummary:
This clinical trial investigates the cognitive and psychological benefits of resistance training for older women. The research team examined how weight-lifting exercises impact cognitive function and mental health in elderly participants. Results show that regular resistance training significantly improved memory scores and reduced depressive symptoms among participants, regardless of whether they lifted heavy or light weights. This finding demonstrates that older women can improve not only physical health but also cognitive abilities and emotional well-being through weight training. Notably, the consistency of exercise itself appears more important than the amount of weight lifted, offering practical implications for fitness programs. However, the study lacks detailed information about participant numbers, exercise duration, and follow-up periods, limiting the generalizability of findings.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 노년 여성의 인지 기능과 정신 건강에 미치는 근력 운동의 효과를 조사했습니다. 연구팀은 임상시험을 통해 저항성 운동(웨이트 트레이닝)이 노년층에게 미치는 영향을 분석했습니다. 연구 결과, 참가자들이 무거운 무게나 가벼운 무게 중 어느 것을 들었든 관계없이 정기적인 저항성 운동은 기억력 점수를 대폭 향상시키고 우울증 증상을 감소시키는 것으로 나타났습니다. 이는 노년 여성들이 단순히 신체 건강뿐만 아니라 인지 능력과 정서적 안정성도 개선할 수 있음을 보여줍니다. 특히 무게의 크기보다는 꾸준한 운동 자체가 중요하다는 점이 실용적 의의를 갖습니다. 다만 구체적인 참가자 수, 운동 기간, 추적 조사 기간 등의 세부 정보가 부족하여 연구의 일반화 가능성을 평가하기 어렵습니다.

articles.title: The surprising reason you’re so productive one day and not the next
articles.titleKo: 어떤 날은 매우 생산적이고 다른 날은 그렇지 않은 놀라운 이유
articles.summary: Feeling mentally “on” isn’t just in your head—it can significantly boost what you accomplish. Researchers found that sharper thinking on a given day leads people to set bigger goals and actually follow through. That edge can equal up to 40 extra minutes of productivity. But push too hard for too long, and the effect reverses.
articles.aiSummary:
This research investigated how daily fluctuations in cognitive performance impact productivity levels. Researchers tracked participants' mental sharpness and its relationship with goal-setting and task completion patterns. The key finding reveals that enhanced cognitive clarity on specific days leads people to establish more ambitious goals and actually achieve them, translating to approximately 40 additional minutes of productivity. The study contributes to understanding the physiological basis of productivity variations, which has implications for self-management strategies. However, while the research confirmed that prolonged excessive effort reverses this positive effect, it did not provide specific methodologies to prevent such burnout. Future research should examine how individual differences and environmental factors moderate these effects.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
